$comment: SecureSpel sandbox restrictions. Each rule is enforced by WorkflowJsonValidator.forbidden_spel_token. The 'pattern' is a Java regex applied to every #{...} expression and every transition.condition. When a match is found the validator emits an error keyed on 'code' with the 'replacement' hint surfaced to the LLM. Update this file in lockstep with SecureSpelEvaluator.java.

rules.description: T(java.lang.Foo) style type references are forbidden by the SpEL sandbox. The evaluator throws 'Access to types is forbidden' at parse time.
rules.replacement: Pre-compute the value in a CodeExecPlugin step (full Java available there) and read it as a workflow variable from SpEL. Common case: T(java.time.LocalDate).now() -> compute todayIso in a CodeExec activity, set it as an output variable.

rules.description: Constructor calls (new Foo(...)) are forbidden by the SpEL sandbox.
rules.replacement: Construct the value in a CodeExecPlugin step and read it via a workflow variable. Inline construction in SpEL is not supported.

rules.description: Spring bean references (@beanName) are forbidden by the SpEL sandbox.
rules.replacement: Beans are not accessible from workflow SpEL. Use AgentToolPlugin / SkillToolPlugin / CodeExecPlugin to reach the capability you need.

rules.description: Calling .getClass() is forbidden by the SpEL sandbox.
rules.replacement: Type inspection is not allowed at SpEL level. Branch on the actual value (e.g. check a field) instead.

rules.description: Reading .class or .classLoader on a SpEL object is forbidden.
rules.replacement: Drop the .class / .classLoader access. Use a field on the value itself.

notes: SpEL inline-list literal: {} is an EMPTY LIST, not an empty Map. To express an empty map use {:} (the colon disambiguates).
Null-safe navigation is allowed and encouraged: #var?.field. The Elvis operator (?:) provides fallback values: (#optional ?: 'default').
Workflow variables are referenced with the # prefix: #myVar. Without the #, the token is interpreted as a property of the root context and will resolve to null.
